titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gene Persson Papers relating to Dutchman
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 9, 2019 at 06:20

did.unittitle: Gene Persson papers relating to Dutchman

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Glenn Horowitz Bookseller, Inc. on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2010.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gene Persson Papers Relating to Dutchman is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gene Persson Papers Relating to Dutchman . James Wheldon Johnson Collection, Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Gene Persson
bioghist.p: Eugene (Gene) Clair Persson (born in Long Beach, California, 1934) was a child actor before becoming a film and theater producer in California and New York. He died in 2008.

bioghist.head: Amiri Baraka
bioghist.p: Amiri Baraka, formerly LeRoi Jones, is an African American writer, critic, and activist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The bulk of the papers relate to theater and film productions of Dutchman by Amiri Baraka, for which Gene Persson served as a producer, including correspondence, scripts, production materials, legal materials, promotional materials, financial records, photographs, and audiovisual materials. The papers also include additional writings and other materials by or about Amiri Baraka.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Dutchman materials, 1960s-2007. II. Non-Dutchman materials, 1964-1991. III. Audiovisual materials, circa 1994.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in rough chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various material relating to Dutchman , including finanical material, correspondence, slides, and photographs.

did.unittitle: Dutchman - The Toilet - The Warner Playhouse

Dutchman - Film Production - Work Permits & Forms

South America, Socialist Countries, Noway, New Zealand - Dutchman Distr.
Diploma of Merit - Edinburgh Film Festival
Dutchman Film Production - Office Bills - Paid
Bills, Financial Statements - N.Y. and London
Bills, Financial Statements - N.Y. and London
Janus Films - Copyright Filing - Richard Gordon Films - Part I - Important Files
Janus Film - Richard Gordon - Part II
Dutchman - Dick Gordon, Kit Parker, distribution, correspondence

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in rough chronological order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains material relating to Amiri Baraka, also known as LeRoi Jones. Also contains additional material, such as catalogs.

did.unittitle: The Toilet (A Play in One Act)

title.part: What Was the Relationship of the Lone Ranger to the Means of Production?

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material is arranged in rough chronological order.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
